mnemonics: List Superbly: Use '-l' for Long, '-a' for All, '-t' for Time.
Alphabetical Order? That's the 'ls' Default, Remember!
keyPoints: The '-l' option provides a detailed long listing, including permissions, owner, size, and modification time.
The '-a' option shows all files, including hidden ones that start with a dot (.).
The '-t' option sorts the list of files by modification time, newest first.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that 'ls' without options does not show hidden files (those starting with a '.').
Confusing the '-l' (long format) option with '-t' (time sort) when trying to see the most recently modified file.
